description_long: This hostel complex consists of three distinct rectangular buildings, each with a pitched, reddish-brown tiled roof and dark gray exterior walls. The largest building is elongated, featuring a series of evenly spaced windows along its length and a raised foundation with visible support columns. The two smaller buildings are more compact, with one appearing square and the other rectangular, both also featuring multiple windows and similar roof styles. The structures are arranged on a flat, gray base, suggesting a courtyard or communal area between them. The windows are symmetrically distributed, and the overall design is utilitarian, prioritizing function over ornate decoration. The buildings evoke a practical, institutional style commonly found in urban residential hostels.
description: A group of three rectangular hostel buildings with reddish-brown tiled roofs and dark gray walls, featuring multiple windows and a functional, institutional design.
description_view_0: From this view, the shorter sides of the two smaller buildings are visible, highlighting the pitched roofs and the window arrangement on the end facades.
description_view_1: This view shows the long side of the largest building, revealing a row of evenly spaced windows and the raised foundation with support columns.
description_view_2: Here, the long side of the central building and the end of the farthest building are visible, emphasizing the symmetry and alignment of the structures.
description_view_3: This view displays the rear or opposite sides of all three buildings, with the square building's roof pattern and the elongated building's length most prominent.
attribute_entrance_design: The main entrance appears to be located on the long side of the largest building, accessible via a raised platform supported by columns. The entrance is likely marked by a set of doors, but specific details such as steps, ramps, or canopies are not clearly visible in the images.
attribute_window_arrangement: Windows are rectangular and symmetrically arranged along the facades of all three buildings. The largest building features a continuous row of evenly spaced windows, while the smaller buildings have fewer but similarly styled windows. No decorative frames or shutters are visible.
attribute_architectural_style: The buildings exhibit a utilitarian, institutional architectural style with minimal ornamentation, flat facades, and functional design elements typical of modern urban hostels.
